message:
feat(tui): add custom extension overlay rendering, input capture, and
lifecycle management in the interactive TUI

Integrate the custom UI hostcall into the interactive terminal interface
with full overlay rendering, keyboard forwarding, and state management.

State (interactive/state.rs):
- Add ExtensionCustomOverlay struct holding the owning extension_id,
  optional title, and latest rendered frame lines
- Derives Debug, Clone, Default for consistency with other overlay types

App state (interactive.rs):
- Add extension_custom_overlay (Option<ExtensionCustomOverlay>),
  extension_custom_active (bool), and extension_custom_key_queue
  (VecDeque<String>) fields to PiApp
- Wire custom key handler into the main key dispatch loop, positioned
  before tree UI to ensure custom overlays capture input first
- Update PiMsg::ExtensionUiRequest doc comment to include "custom"
- Import ExtensionCustomOverlay from state module

Agent dispatch (interactive/agent.rs):
- Route "custom" method requests to handle_custom_extension_ui_request()
  instead of the generic UI queue, since custom overlays use a polling
  protocol rather than one-shot prompts
- Implement handle_custom_extension_ui_request(): handles mode detection
  (poll/close), manages overlay lifecycle, responds with current viewport
  width and queued key input, and signals closed state
- Implement custom_overlay_width_from_payload(): sophisticated width
  negotiation supporting absolute values, percentage strings with
  basis-point precision parsing (e.g., "75.5%"), and terminal-width-aware
  fallback with minimum 20-column floor
- Refactor apply_extension_ui_effect() from a single large match block
  into individual methods: apply_extension_notify_effect(),
  apply_extension_status_effect(), apply_extension_widget_effect(),
  apply_extension_title_effect(), apply_extension_editor_text_effect()
- Add apply_custom_overlay_widget_effect() that intercepts setWidget
  calls using the reserved "__pi_custom_overlay" widget key and routes
  them to the overlay state instead of the message log
- Ensure overlay state is cleaned up when extensions finish or on
  conversation reset
- Guard advance_extension_ui_queue() against custom method requests
  entering the generic queue

Keybindings (interactive/keybindings.rs):
- Add handle_custom_extension_key(): captures all keyboard input when
  a custom overlay is active, encodes it as terminal escape sequences,
  and queues it for the extension; preserves Ctrl+C for global exit
- Add encode_custom_ui_key(): maps the internal KeyMsg/KeyType enum
  to standard terminal escape sequences (ANSI, xterm, Kitty protocol)
  covering runes, control keys, arrows, function keys, page navigation,
  and special keys (Tab, ShiftTab, Enter, Backspace, Delete, etc.)
- Enforce MAX_CUSTOM_KEY_QUEUE (256) to prevent unbounded memory growth
  if the extension polls slowly
- Add tests: custom key input queuing when active, and Ctrl+C passthrough

View rendering (interactive/view.rs):
- Add render_extension_custom_overlay(): renders the overlay with title,
  source extension ID, frame content (tail-windowed to terminal height),
  and a help hint for quitting
- Insert overlay rendering between capability prompt and branch picker
  in the main render pipeline
- Exclude input area rendering when custom overlay is active, matching
  the pattern used by settings, theme picker, and other overlays

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
